This stage sits between raw streaming events and the user-visible transcript, continuously reducing partial output into stable UI state during the main loop. On the parsing side, the stream-parser modules peel assistant text apart as it arrives: `citation.rs` removes hidden citation tags, `proposed_plan.rs` extracts `<proposed_plan>` blocks, and `assistant_text.rs` combines both so visible markdown, citations, and plan segments can advance together. The TUI streaming pipeline then decides what is safe to show: `markdown_stream.rs`, `streaming/mod.rs`, `table_holdback.rs`, `controller.rs`, `chunking.rs`, and `commit_tick.rs` buffer incomplete markdown, avoid prematurely freezing pipe tables, adapt drain speed, and emit committed history cells plus a mutable live tail.

Rendering modules turn that state into terminal lines. `markdown.rs`, `markdown_render.rs`, `table_key_value.rs`, `highlight.rs`, `diff_render.rs`, and `git_action_directives.rs` render markdown, tables, syntax-colored code, diffs, and rewritten hidden directives. `history_cell/*` and `exec_cell/*` define the transcript’s concrete cell types for messages, plans, approvals, exec activity, MCP, notices, hooks, patches, searches, and user-input exchanges. `chatwidget/*`, resize/reflow, status, token, footer, and popup modules keep live cells, status lines, command/hook lifecycles, transcript consolidation, and width-dependent reflow coherent as streaming output evolves.
